# Lucid UI

> Shared React component library for Pandahrms frontend projects. Built with Tailwind CSS v4 (OKLCH color space) and Radix UI primitives. Distributed as the `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui` npm package on **GitHub Packages**.

## Installation

The package is published to **GitHub Packages**, not the public npm registry. Consumers need a project-level `.npmrc`:

```
@pandaworks-sw:registry=https://npm.pkg.github.com
//npm.pkg.github.com/:_authToken=${GITHUB_TOKEN}
```

тАжand a `GITHUB_TOKEN` env var (classic PAT with `read:packages` scope) on every machine and CI job that runs `pnpm install`.

```bash
pnpm add @pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui
```

Then import named components:

```tsx
import { Button, Badge, Modal, StatCard, MeterRow } from '@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui';
```

Peer dependencies the consumer must already have: `react >=19`, `react-dom >=19`, `lucide-react >=0.500`, `react-hook-form >=7`, `tailwindcss >=4`. The package ships its own design-token stylesheet тАФ consumers wire it up in their entry CSS:

```css
@import "tailwindcss";
@import "@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui/styles.css";

@source "../node_modules/@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui";
```

The `styles.css` import provides every token (`--primary`, `--brand`, `--success`, `--info`, `--warning`, `--muted`, etc.), the base layer, and the `text-display-*` / `text-mono-*` / `bg-pattern-*` utilities. The `@source` line lets Tailwind v4 scan the package's compiled JSX so class names used inside components survive tree-shaking. Fonts (Inter, Comfortaa, JetBrains Mono) must be loaded separately by the consumer.

Tree-shaking works out of the box because the package ships ESM with `sideEffects: false`.

### Customizing components

Customizations land upstream in the lucid-ui repo. Don't fork a single component locally тАФ open a PR against lucid-ui or wrap the component in a project-local component that lives outside of `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui`'s exports.

## Tech Stack

- React 19, TypeScript
- Tailwind CSS v4 (OKLCH color space)
- Radix UI primitives
- class-variance-authority for variants
- Lucide icons

## Foundations

The design tokens and typography rules every component is built on. Showcased in the demo under the **Foundations** sidebar group (`/#/colors`, `/#/typography`, `/#/tones`).

### Color tokens

All tokens live in [packages/registry/src/styles.css](../packages/registry/src/styles.css). Use the semantic theme tokens by default тАФ they remap automatically across light and dark.

**Stone scale (cool neutrals)** тАФ `--stone-50` `#F7F9FB` тЖТ `--stone-950` `#080B0E` (50, 100, 150, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700, 800, 900, 950). Tailwind utilities: `bg-stone-{50тАж950}`, `text-stone-*`, `border-stone-*`. Use directly only when you need a specific neutral step; for app surfaces prefer the semantic theme tokens.

**Panda blue (brand accent)** тАФ `--panda-50` `#E7F7FD` тЖТ `--panda-900` `#08323F` (50, 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700, 800, 900). `--brand` and `--ring` resolve to `--panda-400` `#20B0E0`.

**Semantic chroma (non-text fills)** тАФ used for meter bars, status dots, chart fills, icon glyphs:
- `--success` `#4F8A52`
- `--info` `#3D7DD9`
- `--warning` `#C98A2B`
- `--danger` `#C8543F`
- `--destructive` aliases `--danger`

Each has a `--{name}-foreground: #FFFFFF` companion for solid-bg-+-white-text controls.

**WCAG AA pairings (text on tinted bg)** тАФ the chroma tokens cannot pass 4.5:1 against their own tints. For text on `bg-X/15` (light) and `bg-X/20` (dark), use:
- `--success-soft-fg` (light `#377336` / dark `#9FD0A2`) тАФ ~4.7:1 / ~7.4:1
- `--info-soft-fg` (light `#2964C4` / dark `#9FC2F0`) тАФ ~4.7:1 / ~7.2:1
- `--warning-soft-fg` (light `#955B0E` / dark `#F0C47A`) тАФ ~5.2:1 / ~7.2:1
- `--destructive-soft-fg` (light `#A23120` / dark `#F0A896`) тАФ ~5.5:1 / ~7.0:1
- `--destructive-aa: #A8392A` (same in both themes) тАФ solid red bg with white text, 6.3:1

**Adaptive theme tokens (shadcn-style)** тАФ these swap values between light and dark:
- `--background` (stone-50 / stone-800), `--foreground` (stone-800 / stone-150)
- `--card` (white / stone-700), `--popover` (white / stone-700)
- `--primary` (stone-800 / stone-150), `--secondary` (stone-100 / stone-700)
- `--muted` (stone-100 / stone-700), `--accent` (stone-150 / stone-600)
- `--brand` (panda-400, both modes), `--destructive` (chroma --danger, both modes)
- `--border`, `--input` (stone-200 / stone-600); `--input-bg` (stone-100 / stone-800) тАФ field surface for `Input`, `Textarea`, `SelectTrigger`, outline `Button`, `AttachmentInput` compact
- `--ring` (panda-400, both modes)
- `--chart-{1..5}` for data viz palettes
- Sidebar mirror tokens: `--sidebar-background`, `--sidebar-foreground`, `--sidebar-primary`, `--sidebar-accent`, `--sidebar-border`, `--sidebar-ring`

**Rule of thumb:** never pair `bg-X/10` with `text-X` on the same chroma тАФ use the matching `*-soft-fg` token. Reserve raw chroma tokens for non-text fills.

### Typography

Three font families, loaded by the consumer (Google Fonts `<link>` in `index.html`):

| Family | Token | Tailwind utility | Role |
|--------|-------|-------------------|------|
| Inter | `--font-sans` | `font-sans` (default) | Body, headings, labels тАФ every surface that isn't PageHeader or hero |
| Comfortaa | `--font-display` | `font-display` + `text-display-{sm,md,lg,xl}` | `PageHeader` title and hero/marketing surfaces ONLY |
| JetBrains Mono | `--font-mono` | `font-mono` + `text-mono-{sm,md}` | Code, IDs, copyable values, tabular numerics |

**Body scale (Inter)** тАФ token-driven; each utility carries its own line-height and weight:

| Utility | Size | Line-height | Weight | Notes |
|---------|------|-------------|--------|-------|
| `text-xs` | 11px | 1.5 | 600 | letter-spacing 0.04em тАФ eyebrows, badges, captions |
| `text-sm` | 13px | 1.5 | 500 | secondary body, labels, helper text |
| `text-base` | 14px | 1.55 | 400 | default body |
| `text-md` | 15px | 1.55 | 400 | slightly emphasized body |
| `text-lg` | 17px | 1.5 | 500 | section subheading |
| `text-xl` | 20px | 1.45 | 500 | Card / Dialog title |

**Display scale (Comfortaa)** тАФ hero/marketing only:

| Utility | Size | Line-height | Weight | Notes |
|---------|------|-------------|--------|-------|
| `text-display-sm` | 28px | 1.2 | 500 | `PageHeader` title size |
| `text-display-md` | 40px | 1.15 | 600 | hero subhead |
| `text-display-lg` | 56px | 1.1 | 600 | letter-spacing -0.01em |
| `text-display-xl` | 72px | 1.05 | 600 | letter-spacing -0.02em |

**Mono scale (JetBrains Mono):**

| Utility | Size | Line-height | Weight |
|---------|------|-------------|--------|
| `text-mono-sm` | 11px | 1.5 | 400 |
| `text-mono-md` | 13px | 1.5 | 400 |

**Heading font policy (strict)** тАФ see the existing "Heading Font Policy" subsection further down. Comfortaa is reserved for the `PageHeader` title (wired internally тАФ do not re-apply) and the `text-display-*` utilities. Do **not** add a global `h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6 { font-family: var(--font-display) }` rule; the package ships an explicit Inter reset and a downstream global rule defeats it.

### Tones

Shared semantic vocabulary used across `Badge` (variant), `MeterRow` (tone), `ProgressStatCard` items (tone), `StatCard` (deltaTone), and other tone-aware components. **Pick the tone by what the value means, not by what color you want.**

| Tone | Meaning | Badge variant | MeterRow tone | ProgressStatCard cell tone |
|------|---------|---------------|----------------|----------------------------|
| `success` | Live, approved, healthy, on track, paid | `success` | `success` | `success` |
| `warning` | Approaching a soft limit, needs attention, partial | `warning` | `warning` | `warning` |
| `info` | Informational, planning, not started, scheduled | `info` | `info` | `info` |
| `destructive` | Failed, rejected, cancelled, hard limit exceeded, overdue | `destructive` | `destructive` | `destructive` |
| `muted` | Archived, inactive, deprecated | `muted` | `muted` | `muted` |
| `default` / `secondary` | Generic / categorical / goal progress without good-bad meaning | `secondary` (categorical) or `default` (emphasis) | `default` | `default` |

In a breakdown chart of project status (Active / On Hold / Planning / Blocked), match each row's `MeterRow` tone to the corresponding `Badge` tone тАФ pair the same visual language.

`StatCard` `deltaTone` follows **desirability**, not arrow direction:
- Metric went up and that's good (revenue, active users) тЖТ `up`
- Metric went down and that's good (errors, churn, latency) тЖТ also `up` тАФ tone follows desirability
- Metric moved against goal тЖТ `down`
- No meaningful change (within noise) тЖТ `flat`

Stat cell tones in `ProgressStatCard` are graphical emphasis on a count (treated under WCAG SC 1.4.11 / 3:1, not 1.4.3 / 4.5:1). For AA-strict text, pair the cell with a `Badge` or icon and leave the cell tone at `default`.

For full per-component tone tables (incl. when to use `dot`), see [decision-guide.md](docs/decision-guide.md).

## Component Catalog

For full API docs, props, and code examples, read the linked doc file.

### Layout

| Component | Description | Docs |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| AppShell | Data-driven app layout with collapsible sidebar, header, user menu | [app-shell.md](docs/app-shell.md) |
| PageHeader | Page title, description, and action buttons | [layout.md](docs/layout.md) |
| DetailPage | Resource detail/show page layout with sidebar | [detail-page.md](docs/detail-page.md) |
| CardGroup | Responsive grid container for related cards (1тАУ4 cols, auto-collapse, optional shared title + action) | [layout.md](docs/layout.md) |
| EmptyState | Centered empty state with icon, title, description, action | [layout.md](docs/layout.md) |
| Modal | Form modal with sticky header/footer, size variants | [modal.md](docs/modal.md) |
| Stepper | Multi-step progress (horizontal or vertical); descriptions only on current step | [layout.md](docs/layout.md) |
| Separator | Horizontal or vertical divider | [layout.md](docs/layout.md) |
| Breadcrumb | Navigation breadcrumb trail | [layout.md](docs/layout.md) |
| ThemeToggle | Icon-only dropdown that switches the app between light, dark, and system themes; persists the choice in localStorage; ships an `applyStoredTheme()` helper for app-entry no-FOUC bootstrap | [theme-toggle.md](docs/theme-toggle.md) |

### Forms

| Component | Description | Docs |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| Button | Variants, action presets, icon, tooltip, loading | [button.md](docs/button.md) |
| InlineEditableField | Display тЖФ inline-edit toggle with built-in Save/Cancel + `editor` render prop receiving programmatic save/cancel + isSaving | [forms.md](docs/forms.md) |
| MetaEditPill | Click-to-edit inline value pill (label + value-as-DropdownMenu trigger) for fixed option sets. Single mode (Priority / Severity / Module) with optional Clear, and multi mode (Platforms / Tags) backed by checkbox items. Falls back to plain text when `canEdit=false` | [meta-edit-pill.md](docs/meta-edit-pill.md) |
| Input | Text input field | [forms.md](docs/forms.md) |
| NumberInput | Controlled numeric input with min/max clamp, step-driven decimals, suffix, error state, arrow-key stepping | [forms.md](docs/forms.md) |
| PasswordInput | Password field with a built-in show/hide eye toggle; forwards refs and accepts all native input props | [forms.md](docs/forms.md) |
| Label | Accessible form label | [forms.md](docs/forms.md) |
| Textarea | Multi-line text input | [forms.md](docs/forms.md) |
| Checkbox | Checkbox with label support | [forms.md](docs/forms.md) |
| RadioGroup | Radio button group | [forms.md](docs/forms.md) |
| Switch | Toggle switch | [forms.md](docs/forms.md) |
| Select | Static dropdown (< 10 items) | [forms.md](docs/forms.md) |
| SelectPicker | Searchable dropdown, single/multi select | [select-picker.md](docs/select-picker.md) |
| UserPicker | Avatar-trigger picker for assigning one user or many; searchable past 8 users; props include `users`, `mode`, `value`, `onValueChange`, `currentUserId`, `allowUnassigned`, `maxAvatarsShown`, `size` (`xs \| sm \| md \| lg \| xl`), `compact`, `searchable` | [user-picker.md](docs/user-picker.md) |
| DatePicker | Single date picker | [forms.md](docs/forms.md) |
| DateRangePicker | Date range picker with presets | [forms.md](docs/forms.md) |
| Slider | Range slider | [forms.md](docs/forms.md) |
| Form | react-hook-form integration with field context | [form.md](docs/form.md) |
| SearchInput | Debounced search input with clear button | [forms.md](docs/forms.md) |
| SplitButton | Primary action + dropdown secondary actions | [split-button.md](docs/split-button.md) |
| AttachmentInput | File upload with dropzone/compact variants | [attachment-input.md](docs/attachment-input.md) |
| SelectableCard | Card-based single/multi selection | [forms.md](docs/forms.md) |
| FilterBar | FilterButton trigger + ActiveFilters chips | [filter-bar.md](docs/filter-bar.md) |
| SettingsRow | Pure-layout row for /settings/* pages: title + description + helper + control + trailing slot. Inline or stacked layout. Opt-in per-row Save / Cancel via `showSave` so the parent page can run a batched save flow when `showSave` is false (the default) | [settings-row.md](docs/settings-row.md) |
| TagInput | Free-form-and/or-allowlist multi-tag input. Type + Enter/comma to commit, Backspace to remove the last chip. Optional `suggestions` opens an autocomplete popover; `allowFreeForm={false}` locks to the suggestion list. Supports `max`, `validate`, custom `delimiters` | [tag-input.md](docs/tag-input.md) |

### Data Display

| Component | Description | Docs |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| Card | Container with header, content, footer. `CardTitle` accepts `size="sm \| md \| lg"` (default `lg`) for dense / standard / hero cards | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| Table | Data table with header, body, footer | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| Badge | Status indicator with semantic tones (default/secondary/destructive/outline/success/warning/info/muted) and optional dot | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| Avatar | User/entity avatar with image and fallback; `<AvatarFallback>` auto-converts a full-name string to a 2-char monogram and is colorized by default (opt out via `colorize={false}`); supports `shape="circle"` (default) or `"square"` and `size="xs \| sm \| md \| lg \| xl"` (shared scale with AvatarGroup) | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| AvatarGroup | Stack of overlapping Avatars with `max` overflow tile; inherits the Avatar shape; `size` accepts `xs \| sm \| md \| lg \| xl` | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| Calendar | Date calendar | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| Skeleton | Loading placeholder | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| Progress | Progress bar | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| StatCard | Dashboard stat tile (Card + AnimatedNumber + Badge) with delta chip | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| MultiStatCard | Two or more related stat metrics inside one card with dividers between cells; horizontal or vertical orientation | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| ProgressStatCard | Card combining a titled headline, a Progress bar, and a row of tone-aware stat cells (cycle/pipeline/completion summaries) | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| MeterRow | Labeled horizontal bar with per-row tone (success/warning/info/destructive/muted) | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| ListRow | Avatar/icon + title + subtitle + trailing-meta row primitive; supports interactive `asButton` mode | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| Timeline | Vertical event feed with tone-coded dots, optional connector line, title/subtitle/meta slots per item, optional icon override | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| IconBadge | Decorative tone-tinted icon container (rounded/circle/square ├Ч sm/md/lg). Use for hero icons; not a button | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| CodeLabel | Inline monospace label with optional copy button (`copyable` prop) | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| AnimatedNumber | Animated number display with easing | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| ExpandableText | Collapsible long text with show more/less | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| TruncatedLabel | Single- or multi-line label that truncates with ellipsis and shows the full text in a tooltip on hover/focus (tooltip only mounts when truncation is actually happening) | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |

### Feedback

| Component | Description | Docs |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| Alert | Alert banner (default, destructive, info) | [feedback.md](docs/feedback.md) |
| AlertDialog | Modal confirmation dialog | [feedback.md](docs/feedback.md) |
| Sonner | Toast notifications | [feedback.md](docs/feedback.md) |
| LoadingPage | Full-screen loading state | [feedback.md](docs/feedback.md) |
| ConnectionBanner | Offline/connectivity warning banner | [feedback.md](docs/feedback.md) |
| ErrorFallback | Error boundary fallback (root/route variants) | [error-fallback.md](docs/error-fallback.md) |

### Overlay

| Component | Description | Docs |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| Modal | Form modal with sticky header/body/footer (sm / default / lg sizes) | [modal.md](docs/modal.md) |
| AlertDialog | Confirmation prompt (no overlay-click dismiss) | [feedback.md](docs/feedback.md) |
| Sheet | Slide-out panel (top, right, bottom, left) | [overlay.md](docs/overlay.md) |
| DropdownMenu | Context/action menu | [overlay.md](docs/overlay.md) |
| Tooltip | Hover tooltip | [overlay.md](docs/overlay.md) |
| Popover | Click-triggered popover | [overlay.md](docs/overlay.md) |
| Command | Command palette with search | [overlay.md](docs/overlay.md) |

### Navigation

| Component | Description | Docs |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| Tabs | Tab panels (pill and line variants) | [navigation.md](docs/navigation.md) |
| Accordion | Collapsible sections | [navigation.md](docs/navigation.md) |
| Collapsible | Single collapsible section | [navigation.md](docs/navigation.md) |
| Pagination | Page navigation | [navigation.md](docs/navigation.md) |
| ScrollArea | Custom scrollbar container | [navigation.md](docs/navigation.md) |
| Toggle | Single toggle button | [navigation.md](docs/navigation.md) |
| ToggleGroup | Group of toggle buttons | [navigation.md](docs/navigation.md) |

## Decision Guide: Which Component to Use

For **variant / tone / surface / size choices** within a chosen component (e.g. which Badge tone? `sm` vs `default` vs `lg` Modal? `default` vs `sm` Button? human тЖТ circle vs entity тЖТ square Avatar?), see [decision-guide.md](docs/decision-guide.md). The table below picks the *component*; the linked doc picks the *variant*.

| Scenario | Component |
|----------|-----------|
| App-wide layout with sidebar nav | AppShell |
| Page title + description + actions | PageHeader |
| Detail/show page for a resource | DetailPage |
| Form modal with sticky header/footer | Modal |
| Short static dropdown (< 10 items) | Select |
| Searchable dropdown, long lists | SelectPicker |
| Multiple selection from a list | SelectPicker (mode="multiple") |
| Pick a single user (assignee, owner) | UserPicker |
| Pick multiple users (collaborators, watchers) | UserPicker (mode="multi") |
| Pick a single date | DatePicker |
| Pick a date range | DateRangePicker |
| File upload with drag & drop | AttachmentInput (variant="dropzone") |
| Inline file upload button | AttachmentInput (variant="compact") |
| Card-based single/multi selection | SelectableCard |
| Standard action (create/edit/delete) | Button (action prop) |
| Primary + secondary actions | SplitButton |
| Search/filter input with debounce | SearchInput |
| Bounded numeric input (score, percent, hours, weights) | NumberInput |
| Password input with show/hide toggle | PasswordInput |
| Table filtering with chips | FilterButton + ActiveFilters |
| Multi-step wizard progress | Stepper |
| Animated score/metric display | AnimatedNumber |
| Card with one progress + breakdown counts | ProgressStatCard |
| Collapsible long text | ExpandableText |
| Truncated label with hover-to-reveal full text | TruncatedLabel |
| Confirmation before destructive action | AlertDialog |
| Form modal | Modal (use `size="sm"` for compact prompts) |
| Side panel | Sheet |
| Inline monospace ID with copy | CodeLabel |
| Toast notifications | Sonner (toast) |
| Status indicator | Badge |
| Loading placeholder | Skeleton |
| Light/dark/system theme switcher | ThemeToggle |
| Settings page row (label + control + helper + optional save) | SettingsRow |
| Multi-tag input (free-form, suggestions, or both) | TagInput |
| Full-screen loading state | LoadingPage |
| Offline/connectivity warning | ConnectionBanner |
| Error boundary fallback | ErrorFallback |

## Styling Conventions

- Always support dark mode via the `.dark` class (set on `<html>` or an ancestor) plus `dark:` Tailwind variants
- All theme colors are CSS variables defined in `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui/styles.css` (stone neutrals + panda-blue accent + semantic success/info/warning/danger)
- Color space: OKLCH for `bg-pattern-*` utilities; design tokens use HEX for portability
- Icons: Lucide React (`lucide-react`)
- Fonts: Inter (sans), Comfortaa (display), JetBrains Mono (mono) тАФ load via Google Fonts in your `index.html`

### Heading font policy (strict)

Comfortaa (`var(--font-display)`) is reserved for **two surfaces only**:

1. The `<PageHeader>` component title (already wired internally тАФ do not re-apply).
2. The `text-display-sm/md/lg/xl` utilities тАФ large hero/marketing text.

**Everything else in the app renders in Inter (`var(--font-sans)`)** тАФ including all card titles, dialog/modal titles, sheet titles, sidebar section labels, table headers, accordion triggers, page subheadings, `<h2>`/`<h3>`/`<h4>` in user-authored content, etc.

Rules for AI agents and humans editing consumer projects:

- **DO NOT add a global `h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6 { font-family: var(--font-display) }` rule.** Lucid UI's stylesheet now ships an explicit reset that pins headings to Inter; a downstream global heading rule defeats the policy and re-introduces the brand face across the entire app. If you find such a rule in a consumer's `globals.css` / `index.css` / `app.css`, remove it.
- **DO NOT** wrap `<CardTitle>`, `<DialogTitle>`, `<SheetTitle>`, `<AccordionTrigger>`, etc. in `font-display` to "match the page header" тАФ those surfaces are deliberately Inter.
- **DO** add `className="font-display"` (or use a `text-display-*` utility) on a single element when you genuinely need Comfortaa on a marketing/hero surface inside a consumer app. Per-element opt-in only.
- **DO** keep the consumer's CSS import order as documented (`@import "tailwindcss"` тЖТ `@import "@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui/styles.css"` тЖТ consumer styles). Lucid UI's reset must come before consumer overrides for the policy to hold.

## Changelog

Machine-readable changelog for AI agents consuming this library.
When upgrading `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui` in a downstream project, use this section to determine what changed and what actions are required.

Each entry contains:
- **component**: the component name
- **change**: what changed (added | modified | removed | renamed)
- **action**: what consuming projects must do
- **breaking**: whether this is a breaking change
- **details**: concise description of the change

---

### 2026-05-14

#### tag-input тАФ new component (free-form-and/or-allowlist multi-tag input)

- **component**: TagInput
- **change**: added
- **breaking**: no
- **details**: New `TagInput` ships at [packages/registry/registry/default/tag-input/tag-input.tsx](packages/registry/registry/default/tag-input/tag-input.tsx). Controlled `string[]` multi-tag input тАФ the user types in an inline input, `Enter` or `,` commits a chip (configurable via `delimiters`), `Backspace` on an empty input removes the last chip. Each committed value renders as a `Badge variant="secondary"` with a built-in ├Ч remove button (`aria-label="Remove <tag>"`). Three modes coexist in one component: free-form (default), free-form + suggestions (an autocomplete popover anchored to the wrapper opens as the user types, filtered against already-committed values), and allowlist-only (`suggestions` + `allowFreeForm={false}` blocks free-form commits with an inline error). Optional `max` caps the list; optional `validate(value): string | null` blocks invalid commits with an inline error. Commas inside pasted text split into multiple tags. Duplicates and empty strings are blocked silently. Props: `value`, `onChange`, `suggestions`, `allowFreeForm`, `max`, `validate`, `delimiters`, `placeholder`, `disabled`, `className`, `name`, `id`, `aria-invalid`, `aria-describedby`. The wrapper element matches the `Input` chrome (border, shadow, focus ring, `bg-input-bg`) and receives `data-slot="tag-input"`. Built on `Badge` + `Popover` (no Command тАФ the suggestion list is a flat `<button>` list to avoid stealing focus from the inline input).
- **action**: optional тАФ projects with hand-rolled "chip rows + free-form input" combos (skills, recipients, labels, dimensions) can migrate to `TagInput`. Decide between SelectPicker (`mode="multiple"`, fixed list, no free-form) vs TagInput (free-form OR mixed) using the [decision guide](docs/decision-guide.md). Net-new API, nothing else changed.

#### settings-row тАФ new component (pure-layout row for /settings/* pages)

- **component**: SettingsRow
- **change**: added
- **breaking**: no
- **details**: New `SettingsRow` ships at [packages/registry/registry/default/settings-row/settings-row.tsx](packages/registry/registry/default/settings-row/settings-row.tsx). Pure-layout row for settings pages: composes `title` + optional `description` + control (children) + optional `helperText` + optional `trailing` action slot. Two layouts via the `layout` prop: `inline` (default тАФ control sits to the right of the label block, good for `Switch` / icon-trigger controls) and `stacked` (control sits on its own row below, good for full-width `Input` / `Textarea`). Save UI is opt-in via `showSave` (default `false`); when `true`, the row renders a right-aligned `Cancel` + `Save` pair backed by `Button action="cancel"` / `action="save"` so icons and variants stay consistent across the app. The parent owns the state and passes `onSave` / `onCancel` / `dirty` / `saving` / `saveLabel` / `cancelLabel`. `Cancel` is only rendered when `onCancel` is provided. The root receives `data-slot="settings-row"` + `data-layout="<inline|stacked>"` for theming hooks. The default `showSave={false}` keeps the row layout-only so the parent page can run a batched save flow elsewhere (e.g. a footer Save button) тАФ opt in per-row when the row is independent.
- **action**: optional тАФ settings pages with hand-rolled `<div class="flex items-center justify-between">title + control` rows can migrate to `SettingsRow` for consistent spacing, font sizes, and per-row save behavior. New API; nothing else changed.

#### theme-toggle тАФ new component (light/dark/system theme switcher)

- **component**: ThemeToggle
- **change**: added
- **breaking**: no
- **details**: New `ThemeToggle` ships at [packages/registry/registry/default/theme-toggle/theme-toggle.tsx](packages/registry/registry/default/theme-toggle/theme-toggle.tsx). Icon-only `DropdownMenu` trigger with three radio items тАФ `light` (Sun), `dark` (Moon), `system` (Monitor). The active mode is reflected by the trigger icon; selecting a menu item updates `localStorage` (configurable `storageKey`, default `"lucid-theme"`) and toggles the `.dark` class on `<html>`. When the mode is `system`, the component listens for `prefers-color-scheme` changes so the page tracks the OS preference live. Props: `storageKey`, `align` (forwarded to `DropdownMenuContent`, default `"end"`), and all remaining `ButtonProps` except `onClick` / `children` / `asChild` / `action` / `icon` / `loading`. Defaults: `variant="outline"`, `size="icon"`. Also exports a named helper `applyStoredTheme(storageKey?)` that consumers can call in their `main.tsx` before React mounts to avoid a flash of incorrect theme. The demo's hand-rolled 2-state `theme-toggle.tsx` is removed; the four demo call sites (`App.tsx`, `showcase-sidebar.tsx`, `saas-app.tsx`, `pure-app.tsx`) now import the registry export, which closes the pure-showcase `ThemeToggle` registry gap.
- **action**: optional тАФ consumers wanting a richer in-product theme switcher can import `ThemeToggle` and (if they want no-FOUC bootstrap) call `applyStoredTheme()` in their app entry before `createRoot(...).render(<App />)`. Existing import sites are unaffected тАФ `ThemeToggle` is a net-new export and nothing else changed.

---

### 2026-05-12

#### alert-dialog тАФ show/hide animation now slides up from the bottom

- **component**: AlertDialogContent
- **change**: modified
- **breaking**: no
- **details**: `AlertDialogContent` adds `data-[state=open]:slide-in-from-bottom-8` and `data-[state=closed]:slide-out-to-bottom-8` alongside the existing `fade-in/out-0` + `zoom-in/out-95` pair. The dialog rises from the lower viewport edge to its centred resting position and slides back down on close, matching `Modal`'s motion so confirmation prompts feel like part of the same modal family. Existing `duration-200`, overlay fade, focus trap, scroll lock, and the centred `translate-x-[-50%] translate-y-[-50%]` resting position are unchanged.
- **action**: none тАФ visual-only change. No props, exports, or composition rules changed.

#### modal + dialog тАФ Dialog removed from public exports; Modal gains `size` prop (BREAKING)

- **component**: Modal (gains `size`), Dialog (removed)
- **change**: modified (Modal) + removed (Dialog)
- **breaking**: **yes** тАФ Dialog, DialogContent, DialogHeader, DialogFooter, DialogTitle, DialogDescription, DialogTrigger, DialogClose, DialogOverlay, and DialogPortal are no longer exported from `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui`. Import sites must migrate to Modal (form prompts) or AlertDialog (confirmations).
- **details**: `ModalContent` gains `size?: 'sm' | 'default' | 'lg'` (default `default`) so Modal can cover Dialog's old compact footprint without a className override. `sm` renders `max-w-lg` (тЙИ 32rem / 512px тАФ matches the removed `Dialog`'s width), `default` renders `sm:min-w-4xl max-w-4xl` (тЙИ 56rem / 896px тАФ current Modal default), `lg` renders `sm:min-w-5xl max-w-6xl` (тЙИ 72rem / 1152px тАФ data-heavy modals). `ModalContentProps` and `ModalSize` are exported for type annotations; the root receives `data-slot="modal-content"` + `data-size="<size>"`. The Dialog primitive file remains internal to the package because Modal is built on top of Radix Dialog + the shared `DialogPortal` / `DialogOverlay` / `DialogTrigger` / `DialogClose` re-exports тАФ those names are now package-private and can only be imported via the Modal API. `Sheet` and `AlertDialog` are unchanged.
- **action**: migrate every `import { Dialog, DialogContent, ... } from '@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui'` site. For form prompts, swap to `Modal` and pass `size="sm"` to keep the same `max-w-lg` footprint; replace `<DialogHeader><DialogTitle>...</DialogTitle><DialogDescription>...</DialogDescription></DialogHeader>` with `<ModalHeader title="..." description="..." />` and wrap body content in `<ModalBody>`. For confirmation prompts (delete, discard, etc.), migrate to `AlertDialog` instead. See [docs/modal.md](docs/modal.md) тАФ the bottom of that file ships an old тЖТ new mapping table.

#### dialog тАФ open motion is fade + zoom from center (top-left slide removed)

- **component**: DialogContent
- **change**: fixed
- **breaking**: no
- **details**: `DialogContent` drops four directional slide classes that were drifting alongside the fade + zoom тАФ `data-[state=open]:slide-in-from-left-1/2`, `data-[state=open]:slide-in-from-top-[48%]`, `data-[state=closed]:slide-out-to-left-1/2`, and `data-[state=closed]:slide-out-to-top-[48%]`. The dialog is already centred via `translate(-50%, -50%)`, so the diagonal slide combined with `zoom-in-95` made the panel visibly grow from a point near the top-left of the viewport before landing at center. The runtime now matches the docs at https://pandaworks-sw.github.io/lucid-ui/docs/dialog.md: fade + zoom only. `fade-in-0` / `fade-out-0`, `zoom-in-95` / `zoom-out-95`, `duration-200`, the close-button affordance, the overlay backdrop blur, and the resting position are unchanged. `Modal` (`slide-in-from-bottom-8`) and `Sheet` are intentionally untouched тАФ only `Dialog` was drifting.
- **action**: none тАФ visual fix. No props, exports, or composition rules changed. Consumers on `0.8.1` who were relying on the diagonal slide for any reason can re-add the four classes via `className` on `DialogContent`, but the fade + zoom is the documented motion.

#### project тАФ plugin marketplace install command fixed (no registry change)

- **component**: (project doc / demo asset, not a registry component)
- **change**: fixed
- **breaking**: no
- **details**: The demo "Use with Claude Code" install block now shows `/plugin marketplace add https://github.com/pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui.git` instead of the shorthand `/plugin marketplace add pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui`. The shorthand does not resolve in every Claude Code version; the explicit `.git` URL works across versions. Both the rendered install snippet and the `REPO_URL` constant in `apps/demo/src/showcase/skills-view.tsx` are updated together.
- **action**: consumers should use the new explicit URL when installing the plugin: `/plugin marketplace add https://github.com/pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui.git` then `/plugin install lucid-ui@lucid-ui`.

### 2026-05-12

#### project тАФ logo + favicon added (no registry change)

- **component**: (project asset, not a registry component)
- **change**: added
- **breaking**: no
- **details**: Adds the official lucid-ui logo (transparent-background "L" mark + wordmark) at `apps/demo/public/lucid-ui-logo.png` (500├Ч500 PNG) plus a full favicon set in the same folder: `favicon.ico` (multi-size), `favicon-16.png`, `favicon-32.png`, `favicon-48.png`, `icon-256.png`, and `apple-touch-icon.png` (180├Ч180). The demo `<head>` registers each PNG with an explicit `sizes` attribute alongside the ICO so Chromium / Firefox / Safari each pick the sharpest variant for tab strips, bookmark bars, retina home-screens, and the iOS pinned tile. The showcase sidebar header gains an `<img>` of the mark at `h-7 w-7` next to the existing "Lucid UI" wordmark and "Registry" badge (`alt=""` + `aria-hidden="true"` because the wordmark text is the accessible name). README.md leads with a centred logo block above the H1.
- **action**: none for consumers тАФ the logo is a project asset, no registry exports changed. Downstream apps continue to ship their own favicon and brand assets.

#### meta-edit-pill тАФ new component (click-to-edit inline value pill)

- **component**: MetaEditPill
- **change**: added
- **breaking**: no
- **details**: Inline click-to-edit value pill that pairs a static label with a `DropdownMenu`-driven value (`<label> <value-trigger>`). Single-select mode (Priority / Severity / Module) with optional `allowClear` + `clearLabel` items at the top of the menu so users can reset to `null`; multi-select mode (Platforms / Tags) backed by `DropdownMenuCheckboxItem` so the menu stays open between toggles. Falls back to plain `<label> <value>` text when `canEdit=false` so the same JSX renders for viewer-only roles. Trigger uses `text-muted-foreground` on hover-to-`text-foreground` + `bg-muted/60` highlight + a small `ChevronDown` affordance to signal interactivity. Contrast: label `font-medium text-foreground` vs value `text-muted-foreground` at `text-sm` measures 9.0:1 in light mode and 8.8:1 in dark mode тАФ both AA pass. Props: `label`, `mode` ('single' | 'multi'), `value`, `options`, `onChange`, `canEdit`, `disabled`, `emptyText`, `allowClear` + `clearLabel` (single only), `className`. Designed for detail-page meta strips where multiple fields share one line (ticket priority / severity / module / platforms, project status / phase / type, etc.). Reuses the existing `DropdownMenu` primitive тАФ no new dependencies.
- **action**: import `MetaEditPill` from `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui` and wire `onChange` to your mutation hook; pass `disabled` while the mutation is pending. Compose several pills in a `flex flex-wrap items-center gap-x-8 gap-y-2 text-sm` container to build a meta strip.

#### modal тАФ entry/exit animation slides from the bottom instead of the top-left

- **component**: ModalContent
- **change**: modified
- **breaking**: no
- **details**: `ModalContent` swaps the diagonal `slide-out-to-left-1/2 + slide-out-to-top-[48%]` (and matching `slide-in-from-*`) pair for a single vertical axis: `data-[state=open]:slide-in-from-bottom-8` and `data-[state=closed]:slide-out-to-bottom-8`. The modal now rises from the lower viewport edge to its centred resting position and slides back down on close тАФ matches the sheet pattern users already see on mobile flows and feels less like a tooltip animation. Existing `fade-in-0` / `fade-out-0`, `zoom-in-95` / `zoom-out-95`, `duration-200`, overlay fade, focus trap, scroll lock, and the centred `translate-x-[-50%] translate-y-[-50%]` resting position are unchanged. The 32px (`bottom-8`) travel stays inside the viewport even at the existing `max-h-[85vh]` ceiling on short windows.
- **action**: none тАФ visual-only change. No props, exports, or composition rules changed.

#### avatar тАФ adds `xl` size; AvatarGroup and UserPicker pick it up automatically

- **component**: Avatar, AvatarGroup, UserPicker
- **change**: modified
- **breaking**: no
- **details**: Extends `AvatarSize` from `'xs' | 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg'` to `'xs' | 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' | 'xl'`. The new `xl` token renders at `size-16 text-lg` (64px), one step above `lg` (48px). The `avatarSizeClass` map exported from the `avatar` module gains the `xl` entry, so every consumer of the shared scale picks it up: `AvatarGroup` gets `xl: '-space-x-4'` overlap spacing and `UserPicker` forwards `size="xl"` directly to its trigger Avatar / AvatarGroup. Contrast and shape behaviour are unchanged тАФ same colorize logic, same `circle` / `square` shapes, same fallback rules. Use `xl` for profile headers, settings page member identity, and oversized assignment triggers where the existing `lg` (48px) reads as too small next to surrounding form-row text.
- **action**: optional тАФ pass `size="xl"` on `Avatar`, `AvatarGroup`, or `UserPicker` where a 64px avatar fits the design. No migration required for existing call sites.

#### card тАФ `CardTitle` gains `size` scale (`sm` / `md` / `lg`)

- **component**: CardTitle
- **change**: modified
- **breaking**: no
- **details**: `CardTitle` now accepts `size?: 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg'` (default `'lg'`) so consumers can pick a title scale by surrounding card density without overriding `className`. `sm` renders `text-base font-semibold` (16px) for dense tile-sized cards in 3-up / 4-up `CardGroup` layouts; `md` renders `text-lg font-semibold tracking-tight` (18px) for standard product cards in dashboards or detail pages; `lg` (default) preserves the historical `text-2xl font-semibold leading-none tracking-tight` (24px) for hero / single-card surfaces. `CardTitleSize` is exported for type annotations and the root element gets `data-slot="card-title"` + `data-size="<size>"` for theming hooks. `icon` and `trailing` slots compose unchanged at every size. Plain `<CardTitle>Text</CardTitle>` usage is unchanged. Replaces the `<CardTitle className="text-base">` override pattern that was sprinkled through color-tinted card grids and dense dashboards. Contrast / typography unchanged тАФ Inter at all sizes, no Comfortaa (heading font policy preserved).
- **action**: optional тАФ replace `<CardTitle className="text-base">` with `<CardTitle size="sm">` and `<CardTitle className="text-lg">` with `<CardTitle size="md">`. Existing call sites continue to work without changes.

---

### 2026-05-11

#### truncated-label тАФ new component (ellipsis label with hover-to-reveal full text)

- **component**: TruncatedLabel
- **change**: added
- **breaking**: no
- **details**: Single- or multi-line label that truncates with ellipsis when its text overflows the parent container and shows the full text in a `Tooltip` on hover or focus. Truncation is detected via `ResizeObserver` + `scrollWidth > clientWidth` (single line) or `scrollHeight > clientHeight` (`lines > 1`, using `-webkit-line-clamp`), so the tooltip is only mounted when truncation is actually happening. Props: `text: string`, `lines?: number` (default 1), `side` / `align` / `delayDuration` for tooltip placement, `disableTooltip?: boolean` to skip the popup even when truncated. When truncated and the tooltip is enabled, the rendered `<span>` becomes keyboard-focusable (`tabIndex={0}`, override-able by passing your own `tabIndex`) so keyboard users can trigger the popup. Requires a `<TooltipProvider>` in the tree (already at the `AppShell` root). Replaces the `<span className="truncate" title={...}>` pattern for table cells, list rows, breadcrumbs, file paths, and long contact names.
- **action**: optional тАФ replace local `truncate + title` spans with `TruncatedLabel` when you want a styled tooltip instead of the native browser title.

#### user-picker тАФ new component (avatar-trigger user assignment picker)

- **component**: UserPicker
- **change**: added
- **breaking**: no
- **details**: Avatar-trigger picker for assigning one user (single mode) or many (multi mode). Composes `Popover` + `Command` + `Avatar` + `AvatarGroup`; exposes discriminated-union `mode` props (`mode?: 'single'` with `value: string | null`, or `mode: 'multi'` with `value: string[]`); auto-shows Command search when `users.length > 8`; and uses `AvatarGroup` overflow for the multi-trigger. Replaces the hand-rolled `Select` + `Avatar` pattern for assignee, owner, collaborator, reviewer, and watcher fields.
- **action**: optional тАФ replace local Select + Avatar assignee controls with `UserPicker`

#### user-picker тАФ `currentUserId` pins and labels the active user

- **component**: UserPicker
- **change**: modified
- **breaking**: no
- **details**: Added optional `currentUserId?: string`. When it matches a user, that user is rendered first in the dropdown list (below the single-mode `Unassigned` row when present) and its visible menu-row label is `Me`; the trigger avatar/title and Command search value continue to use the real user name and email. Works in single and multi modes.
- **action**: optional тАФ pass `currentUserId` when assignment menus should make the signed-in user easy to pick

#### user-picker тАФ trigger hover affordance

- **component**: UserPicker
- **change**: modified
- **breaking**: no
- **details**: Enabled picker triggers now add a token-based hover ring (`hover:ring-2 hover:ring-ring/40 hover:ring-offset-2 hover:ring-offset-background`) with `cursor-pointer`, preserving the existing focus-visible ring and disabled styling. No background fill is added.
- **action**: none required.

#### avatar тАФ `size` + `compact` props; shared size scale with AvatarGroup

- **component**: Avatar, AvatarGroup
- **change**: modified
- **breaking**: no
- **details**: `Avatar` now accepts `size?: 'xs' | 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg'` (xs `size-5 text-[10px]`, sm `size-7 text-xs`, md `size-9 text-sm`, lg `size-12 text-base`) тАФ the same scale `AvatarGroup` was carrying internally. The shared map is now exported from the `avatar` module as `avatarSizeClass`, and the `AvatarSize` type is exported alongside `AvatarShape`. `AvatarGroup` imports the shared map instead of duplicating it. `compact?: boolean` on `Avatar` is a shorthand for `size="xs"` for dense surfaces (table rows, inline list cells) and wins over `size` when both are set. Omitting `size` keeps the historical `h-10 w-10` default тАФ existing `<Avatar>` call sites are unchanged.
- **action**: none тАФ additive. New `size` / `compact` props are optional.

#### user-picker тАФ `size` aligns with AvatarSize; gains `compact` shorthand

- **component**: UserPicker
- **change**: modified
- **breaking**: no
- **details**: `size` accepts `'xs' | 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg'` (was `'sm' | 'md' | 'lg'`) and forwards directly to `Avatar` / `AvatarGroup` тАФ no internal remap. `compact?: boolean` is the same shorthand-for-`xs` rule used on `Avatar`, intended for dense table rows. The local `AVATAR_SIZE_CLASS` / `AVATAR_GROUP_SIZE` / `COMPACT_AVATAR_GROUP_SIZE` constants are gone; the picker has one source of truth for sizing via `AvatarSize`. The previous `sm | md | lg` API keeps working, but its rendered pixel sizes now match `AvatarGroup` exactly (`sm` `size-7`, `md` `size-9`, `lg` `size-12` instead of `size-6 / size-8 / size-10`), so a single-mode trigger and a multi-mode `AvatarGroup` of the same size are visually identical.
- **action**: none required. If you were relying on the older pixel sizes (`size-6`/`size-8`/`size-10`) for the single-mode trigger, the avatar is now 1 step larger at each token; pass `compact` (or `size="xs"`) for the smallest footprint, or wrap the picker in your own size-overriding `className` if needed.

### 2026-05-07

#### timeline тАФ new component (vertical activity feed with tone-coded dots)

- **component**: Timeline
- **change**: added
- **breaking**: no
- **details**: Vertical event-feed primitive with a connector line and tone-coded dots per item. Each `TimelineItem` exposes `id`, `tone` ('default' | 'info' | 'success' | 'warning' | 'destructive' | 'muted'), `title`, optional `subtitle`, optional `meta` (right-aligned trailing slot), and an optional `icon` override that replaces the dot. Top-level props: `items`, `defaultTone`, `hideConnector` (single-event compact mode), `emptyState`. The connector line uses `bg-border` (decorative); dots use semantic-tone backgrounds with a 4px `ring-background` so they read against any card surface. Use for activity streams, audit logs, changelog histories.
- **action**: none тАФ additive

#### icon-badge тАФ new component (decorative tone-tinted icon container)

- **component**: IconBadge
- **change**: added
- **breaking**: no
- **details**: Decorative icon container with semantic tone variants (`default`, `info`, `success`, `warning`, `destructive`, `muted`, `primary`) ├Ч size (`sm` 32px / `md` 48px / `lg` 64px) ├Ч shape (`rounded` xl / `circle` full / `square` md). Tones use 10тАУ15% chroma tints so they pair with any surface. Children are rendered through a fixed-size aria-hidden inner span so consumer Lucide icons inherit the right size. Not a button тАФ for clickable surfaces use `Button`. Use for centered hero icons on auth pages, setup wizards, status flags, empty states.
- **action**: none тАФ additive

#### card-group тАФ new component (responsive related-card grid)

- **component**: CardGroup
- **change**: added
- **breaking**: no
- **details**: Responsive grid container for groups of related `Card`s with consistent gutters. `columns: 1 | 2 | 3 | 4` (auto-collapses to 1 on mobile, 2 on `md`, target columns on `xl`). `gap: 'sm' | 'default' | 'lg'`. Optional `title` (rendered as a small `<h3>`) and `action` (right-aligned trailing slot) render a shared header above the grid. Replaces the ad-hoc `<div className="grid grid-cols-1 gap-4 md:grid-cols-2 xl:grid-cols-3">` pattern that consumer code keeps re-creating.
- **action**: none тАФ additive

#### inline-editable-field тАФ new component (display тЖФ inline edit toggle)

- **component**: InlineEditableField
- **change**: added
- **breaking**: no
- **details**: Single field that toggles between a display value (with an Edit affordance) and an inline editor (with built-in Save / Cancel buttons). Manages the `editing` and `isSaving` state internally. The `editor` is a render prop receiving `{ save, cancel, isSaving }` so the consumer can wire Enter/Escape and disable the editor while save is in flight. Props: `label`, `display`, `emptyText` (rendered when `display` is empty), `editor`, `onSave` (sync or async тАФ component awaits the promise to drive `isSaving`), `onCancel`, `disabled` (hides the Edit button entirely), `editLabel` (override "Edit"). Replaces the hand-rolled "Card тЖТ CardHeader (label + Edit button) тЖТ CardContent (paragraph or Textarea + Save/Cancel)" pattern.
- **action**: none тАФ additive

#### card тАФ `CardTitle` gains optional `icon` and `trailing` props

- **component**: CardTitle
- **change**: modified
- **breaking**: no
- **details**: `CardTitle` now accepts optional `icon` (leading) and `trailing` (right-aligned slot) props. When set, the title automatically becomes a flex row with consistent spacing тАФ replacing the ad-hoc `<CardTitle className="flex items-center gap-2"><Icon /> Title</CardTitle>` pattern that was sprinkled across consumer code. Both props are `ReactNode`. Existing `<CardTitle>Plain text</CardTitle>` usage is unchanged. Icon renders in `text-muted-foreground` for visual consistency.
- **action**: optional тАФ replace `<CardTitle className="flex items-center gap-2"><Icon /> Title</CardTitle>` patterns with `<CardTitle icon={<Icon />}>Title</CardTitle>`

### 2026-05-04

#### foundations тАФ Colors / Typography / Tones showcase pages + llms.txt section

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: |
    Added a new "Foundations" sidebar group to the demo with three pages:
      - `/#/colors` тАФ stone scale, panda blue scale, semantic chroma, WCAG AA pairings (success/info/warning/destructive soft-fg + destructive-aa), adaptive theme tokens (background, foreground, card, primary, secondary, muted, accent, brand, destructive, border, input, input-bg, ring), and chart tokens. Each swatch shows the token name plus a click-to-copy hex/value caption.
      - `/#/typography` тАФ the three font families (Inter / Comfortaa / JetBrains Mono) with sample text, the body / display / mono size scales (size, line-height, weight, letter-spacing), and a "Heading font policy (strict)" callout with correct/incorrect examples.
      - `/#/tones` тАФ shared semantic-tone palette table (success / warning / info / destructive / muted / default) with paired Badge + MeterRow rendering, plus live demos of how `Badge`, `MeterRow`, `ProgressStatCard`, and `StatCard` `deltaTone` consume the tones.

    No new tokens, components, or utilities introduced тАФ these pages document tokens that already shipped. New showcase files: `apps/demo/src/showcase/demos/colors-demo.tsx`, `typography-demo.tsx`, `tones-demo.tsx`. The showcase app's `CATEGORIES` array gains a leading "Foundations" group so they appear above the component catalog in the sidebar.

    `public/llms.txt` gains a new "Foundations" section between the Tech Stack table and the Component Catalog, listing every color token (with hex), the three font families with their utilities, both size scales, and the tone vocabulary cross-referenced to the components that consume it. The decision-guide reference is preserved as the canonical "which Badge variant?"-style lookup.
- action:
  - No consumer action required тАФ these are documentation surfaces.
  - When choosing a color: reach for the adaptive theme tokens first (`bg-background`, `bg-card`, `bg-muted`, `bg-primary`, `bg-brand`); reach for raw stone/panda/chroma steps only when you genuinely need a specific neutral or non-text fill.
  - When pairing text on a tinted-bg control (badge, banner, chip), use `text-success-soft-fg` / `text-info-soft-fg` / `text-warning-soft-fg` / `text-destructive-soft-fg` rather than the chroma `text-success` / etc. тАФ only the soft-fg tokens clear AA on `bg-X/15` (light) and `bg-X/20` (dark).
  - When picking a tone for a Badge/MeterRow/ProgressStatCard cell/StatCard delta, pick by meaning (success = healthy, warning = needs attention, etc.) and match the same tone across paired components in a single chart.

---

#### password-input тАФ new component

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: |
    New `PasswordInput` thin wrapper around `Input` that renders an Eye / EyeOff icon button inside the field (right side, `pr-9` reserved on the input). Clicking the toggle flips the input's `type` between `password` and `text`. Forwards refs to the underlying input via `forwardRef` so it composes with `react-hook-form` register and other ref-based form libs. Accepts every standard `<input>` prop except `type` (owned by the toggle), plus `defaultVisible?: boolean` for one-time / temporary-password fields and `toggleLabel?: { show: string; hide: string }` for localized aria-labels.

    The toggle button sits in the natural tab order (no `tabIndex={-1}`), uses `aria-pressed` semantics, has a stateful `aria-label` ("Show password" / "Hide password"), gets `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring`, and disables in lockstep with the input's `disabled` prop.

    Visually it reuses the existing `--input-bg` form surface and the standard `focus-visible:ring-ring` focus treatment тАФ no new design tokens introduced. Icon comes from `lucide-react` (already a peer dep), so no new dependency added.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui` to pick up `PasswordInput`.
  - Replace any local "Input + manual eye-toggle button" wrapper with `<PasswordInput value={value} onChange={...} autoComplete="current-password" />`. For new-password screens, pass `autoComplete="new-password"`.
  - For temporary-password / OTP-style reveal-by-default fields, pass `defaultVisible`.

---

#### number-input тАФ new component

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: |
    New `NumberInput` controlled component that wraps `Input` + `Label`. Models the empty state as `value: number | ''` (matching what `<input value>` accepts) so callers don't have to translate `undefined` тЖФ `''`. Supports `minimum` / `maximum` inclusive bounds with clamping, `step` (defaults to `1`) which also drives `toFixed(decimalPlaces)` formatting on blur, an inline `suffix` rendered inside the field (`%`, `h`, `x`, ...) with the input padded to `pr-12`, тЖС/тЖУ keyboard stepping that clamps to bounds, and a non-passive `wheel` listener that prevents accidental value changes when the page scrolls with a focused input.

    Two messaging surfaces below the field: a transient info message (`text-info`, ~3s auto-dismiss) appears when blur clamps the typed value to `minimum`/`maximum`, and a persistent destructive message appears when the parent passes `error`. The persistent error suppresses the transient/helper text. The error state also flips the input to `aria-invalid` plus a `border-destructive` + `focus-visible:ring-destructive/40` outline.

    Imported from the Pandahrms-Performance project's local `src/components/ui/number-input.tsx` so multiple consumer apps can share the same constraint behavior. The validation helper lives in a sibling `number-input-validator.ts` so it can be unit-tested in isolation; it is not re-exported from the package barrel.

    Visually it reuses the existing `--input-bg` form surface and the `aria-invalid` / `border-destructive` error treatment, so it inherits the same dark-mode tokens as the rest of the form-fields family тАФ no new design tokens introduced.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui` to pick up `NumberInput`.
  - Replace any local hand-rolled "Input + decimal step + clamp + suffix + error" wrapper with `<NumberInput value={value} onChange={setValue} minimum={n} maximum={n} step={n} suffix="..." error={error} />`. If the consumer was using `value: number | undefined`, switch to `number | ''` (callers should map `undefined` тЖТ `''` before passing in).

---

### 2026-05-03

#### form-fields тАФ fields and outline buttons now render on a tinted `--input-bg` surface

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: |
    Added a new `--input-bg` design token (light: `var(--stone-100)` `#EFF3F6`, dark: `var(--stone-800)` `#1C2229`) and switched the field surface on `Input`, `Textarea`, `SelectTrigger`, outline `Button`, and `AttachmentInput`'s compact trigger from `bg-background` to `bg-input-bg`. Pickers (`DatePicker`, `DateRangePicker`, `SelectPicker`) inherit through `Button` outline; `SearchInput` inherits through `Input`.

    Previously every form surface used `bg-background` (`stone-50` `#F7F9FB` light / `stone-800` dark). In light mode that sat at ~1.02:1 against the `#FFFFFF` `Card`/`Dialog` surface, so inputs and outline buttons inside panels effectively blended in and only the 1px border carried the boundary. The new tint sits at a visible offset against both white cards and the page bg, while still reading as a "field" rather than a heavy filled control.

    Dark mode is intentionally unchanged in appearance тАФ `--input-bg: var(--stone-800)` resolves to the same value `bg-background` was already producing in dark mode, so the existing pressed-in-field look is preserved (input darker than the stone-700 card surface).

    Contrast: foreground text (`--foreground` stone-800) on `--input-bg` (stone-100) тЙИ 14.4:1 (AAA) light; placeholder (`--muted-foreground` stone-600) on `--input-bg` тЙИ 7.0:1 (AAA) light. Dark-mode contrast is unchanged from prior baseline.

    The `--input` token (input border color, `stone-200` light / `stone-600` dark) is unchanged тАФ this change adds a separate background token rather than repurposing the existing border token. The `@theme inline` block now exposes `--color-input-bg` so the `bg-input-bg` Tailwind utility resolves correctly.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui` to pick up the change.
  - If your consumer overrides `bg-background` on inputs locally (e.g. `Input className="bg-white"`), decide whether to drop that override (and inherit the new token) or keep it (and stay flat-on-card).
  - No code or markup changes required for default usage.

---

#### accordion тАФ animation keyframes + theme tokens shipped (silent no-op fix)

- change: fixed
- breaking: no
- details: |
    `AccordionContent` already applied `data-[state=closed]:animate-accordion-up data-[state=open]:animate-accordion-down` (driven by Radix's `data-state`), but the package never shipped the matching `@keyframes accordion-down` / `@keyframes accordion-up` or the `--animate-accordion-down` / `--animate-accordion-up` `@theme inline` tokens. The animation classes resolved to nothing, so AccordionContent collapsed/expanded instantly with no transition unless the consumer hand-defined the keyframes (against `--radix-accordion-content-height`) in their own `globals.css`.

    Added both keyframes and both animation tokens to [packages/registry/src/styles.css](packages/registry/src/styles.css). Animation now works out of the box.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui` to pick up the keyframes.
  - If your consumer's `globals.css` (or `index.css`) defines `@keyframes accordion-down` / `@keyframes accordion-up` and/or `--animate-accordion-down` / `--animate-accordion-up`, **delete those local copies** тАФ they are now duplicated in the package. Cascade order makes the local copy win silently, but it's redundant and will drift.

---

#### styles тАФ token-driven scrollbar styling shipped in `@layer base`

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: |
    Added Firefox (`scrollbar-width: thin`, `scrollbar-color: var(--stone-300) transparent`, dark-mode `var(--stone-600)`) and WebKit (`::-webkit-scrollbar` 10├Ч10, thumb on `--stone-300`/`--stone-400` light and `--stone-600`/`--stone-500` dark, transparent track, 6px radius) scrollbar rules to the package's base layer. The block uses only existing `--stone-*` tokens, so it tracks the design system's neutral scale automatically.

    Previously every consumer that wanted consistent scrollbar chrome rebuilt these rules locally against the same tokens (Pandahrms-Performance, etc.). Consolidating upstream removes that duplication and prevents drift if the stone scale ever shifts.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui`.
  - Delete any local `* { scrollbar-width: thin; scrollbar-color: var(--stone-300) transparent }` / `::-webkit-scrollbar*` block from your consumer's `globals.css` тАФ the package now provides it. Consumers with project-specific scrollbar overrides continue to win via cascade order; the package only fills the default.

---

#### typography тАФ Comfortaa scoped to PageHeader + display utilities; everything else reverts to Inter

- change: modified
- breaking: no (token names unchanged; visual change only)
- details: |
    Removed the global base-layer rule that applied Comfortaa to every `<h1>`тАУ`<h6>`, and replaced it with an explicit `h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6 { font-family: var(--font-sans) }` reset inside `@layer base`. Comfortaa now renders only on the `PageHeader` title and the `text-display-sm/md/lg/xl` hero/marketing utilities; all other headings, body text, labels, and dialog/card titles render in Inter via `--font-sans`.

    The `--font-display` CSS variable and the `font-display` Tailwind utility are unchanged тАФ both still resolve to Comfortaa. `PageHeader` now applies `font-display` to its title `<h1>` so the brand face stays on that one component without leaking via global selectors. The `text-display-*` utilities continue to use `var(--font-display)`.

    A new "Heading font policy (strict)" section in this file (and a matching block in the project `CLAUDE.md`) documents the rule for AI agents and devs working in downstream consumers: do not add a global `h1тАУh6 { font-family: var(--font-display) }` rule, and remove any stale copy you find in a consumer's `globals.css` / `index.css` / `app.css`.

    No accessibility regression тАФ Inter is already the body face and clears AA at all sizes used in headings; Comfortaa retention on hero text is unchanged.
- action:
  - Audit consumer projects for a stray `h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6 { font-family: var(--font-display) }` block (commonly in `globals.css` or `index.css`). If found, **delete it** тАФ it overrides Lucid UI's reset and re-introduces Comfortaa across the entire app.
  - If your app legitimately needs Comfortaa on a specific heading or hero element, add `className="font-display"` (or use a `text-display-*` utility) on that single element. Per-element opt-in only.
  - Confirm the CSS import order in your consumer entry CSS is `@import "tailwindcss";` then `@import "@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui/styles.css";` then your own styles тАФ the reset only wins if Lucid UI's stylesheet loads before consumer overrides.

---

#### progress-stat-card тАФ new component

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: |
    New `ProgressStatCard` component composes `Card`, `Progress`, and a tone-aware stat-cell row into a single card. Header lays out a left-side `title` with an optional right-side trailing slot тАФ `value` (bold `text-2xl`) for a single percent/number headline, OR `valueHint` (small `text-sm text-muted-foreground`) for ratio-style subtitles like `"5 of 12"`. The `Progress` bar sits below the header. The `items` prop renders 2+ stat cells underneath in a responsive grid (2-column on mobile, `auto-cols-fr grid-flow-col` row on `sm:` and above), each cell styled as `text-xl font-semibold tabular-nums` value over a `text-xs text-muted-foreground` label. Each item accepts an optional `tone` (`default | success | destructive | warning | info | muted`) that swaps the value color via the chroma tokens (`text-success`, `text-destructive`, etc.) тАФ the same pattern hand-rolled across multiple project dashboards.

    The cell tone colors come from the chroma layer (`--success`, `--destructive`, `--warning`, `--info`) and are intended as graphical emphasis on a count, treated under WCAG SC 1.4.11 (Non-text Contrast, 3:1) rather than 1.4.3. The doc page calls this out and recommends `tone="default"` plus a `Badge` or icon when AA-strict text is required.

    Closes the gap surfaced by the Pandahrms-Performance dashboard's `CycleStatusAdmin` / `CycleStatusManager` widgets, which previously hand-rolled a `<div>` card containing `Progress` plus a `<dl>` grid of stat cells. The shape is general enough to cover any "one progress reading + a breakdown of counts" surface (cycle progress, onboarding pipeline, KPI completion band).

    `Card` is composed without `CardHeader`/`CardContent` (their grid layout would force a clumsy override) тАФ the component supplies a single `p-5` padding via className on the outer Card and lays the header / progress / cells out internally. `data-slot="progress-stat-card"` is set for theming hooks. No new design tokens introduced; everything reuses existing chroma + foreground/muted-foreground + Card/Progress styles.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui` to pick up `ProgressStatCard`.
  - Replace hand-rolled `<div className="rounded-lg border bg-card p-5"><h3/><span>%</span><Progress/><dl>cells</dl></div>` cycle/pipeline cards with `<ProgressStatCard title="..." value="..." progress={n} items={[{ label, value, tone }, ...]} />`.
  - For ratio-headline variants (`5 of 12`, `12 of 18`), pass `valueHint` instead of `value`.

---

### 2026-05-02

#### button тАФ fix `asChild` crash (`React.Children.only`)

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: |
    `Button` crashed at render whenever `asChild={true}` was used. The implementation always built children as `[ResolvedIcon && <Icon/>, resolvedChildren]`, so when `Comp` was Radix `Slot` (the `asChild` branch) JSX collapsed children into a 2-item array and `Slot.SlotClone` called `React.Children.only`, throwing `React.Children.only expected to receive a single React element child`. Every `<Button asChild><a>...</a></Button>` and `<Button asChild><Link>...</Link></Button>` consumer hit this on first render.

    Fix in `packages/registry/registry/default/button/button.tsx`: split the render path. When `asChild=true`, render `<Slot>` with only `resolvedChildren` (the consumer's element). The auto-icon, the auto-tooltip wrapper, and `disabled={disabled || loading}` are only applied on the native-button path. The consumer's element owns its own internals тАФ render any icon inside the child element yourself.

    Added an "As Child (link styled as button)" section to the showcase demo (`apps/demo/src/showcase/demos/button-demo.tsx`) covering ghost / outline / default variants with `<a>` children, so a future refactor can't silently re-introduce the crash. `public/docs/button.md` now documents that `icon`, `action`, and `loading` are ignored under `asChild`.

    Variant names, prop shapes, public API, and the native-button render path are unchanged. Previously working call sites keep working; previously broken `asChild` call sites now work.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui` to pick up the fix.
  - No code changes required for consumers. `<Button asChild>` is now safe to use across all variants.
  - If you previously worked around this by applying `buttonVariants(...)` className directly to a `<Link>` / `<a>`, you can now switch to `<Button asChild>` for consistency with the rest of the registry.

#### badge / button / styles тАФ WCAG 2.1/2.2 contrast pass

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: |
    Audited Badge and Button destructive against SC 1.4.3 (Contrast Minimum, 4.5:1 for normal text тАФ Badge text is `text-xs` / 11px / semibold, classified as normal text under WCAG, NOT large text). Multiple variants failed in either or both themes; fixed by introducing scoped tokens that pass AA. The chroma tokens themselves (`--success`, `--info`, `--warning`, `--danger`, `--destructive`, `--border`) are untouched, so any component consuming `text-success` / `bg-warning` / etc. directly continues to render the same color.

    Tokens added in `packages/registry/src/styles.css` (both `:root` and `.dark`):
      - `--success-soft-fg` (light `#377336` / dark `#9fd0a2`) тАФ Badge `text-success-soft-fg` over `bg-success/15` light and `bg-success/20` dark (tints bumped from `/10` and `/15` so the chroma reads on both themes). Pre-fix contrast 3.51 / 3.24. Post-fix ~4.7 / 7.4.
      - `--info-soft-fg` (light `#2964c4` / dark `#9fc2f0`) тАФ same pattern. Pre-fix 3.50 / 3.29. Post-fix ~4.7 / 7.2.
      - `--warning-soft-fg` (light `#955b0e` / dark `#f0c47a`) тАФ same pattern. Pre-fix 2.53 / 4.33 (the worst offender). Post-fix ~5.2 / 7.2.
      - `--destructive-soft-fg` (light `#a23120` / dark `#f0a896`) тАФ reserved for future soft destructive variants; not yet wired.
      - `--destructive-aa: #a8392a` (same in both themes) тАФ solid red bg with white text. Pre-fix `#c8543f` gave 4.38:1; `#a8392a` gives 6.30:1. Used by Badge `destructive` and Button `destructive`.

    Component changes:
      - `packages/registry/registry/default/badge/badge.tsx` тАФ `success` / `warning` / `info` variants now use `text-success-soft-fg` / `text-warning-soft-fg` / `text-info-soft-fg` (was `text-success` / `text-warning` / `text-info`). `destructive` variant now uses `bg-destructive-aa` + `hover:bg-destructive-aa/90` (was `bg-destructive` + `hover:bg-destructive/80`). `muted` variant adds `dark:text-stone-300` (was `text-muted-foreground` only тАФ `stone-400` on `stone-700` measured 4.24:1; `stone-300` is ~7.3:1).
      - `packages/registry/registry/default/button/button.tsx` тАФ `destructive` variant now uses `bg-destructive-aa` (was `bg-destructive`). The `delete` action preset (`<Button action="delete">`) inherits the fix.

    Variant names, prop shapes, and component APIs are unchanged. Visual change is intentional and minor: destructive controls are a slightly darker red, and tinted-bg badges have a slightly darker (light mode) / lighter (dark mode) text color. `MeterRow`/`Alert`/`DropdownMenu` etc. that consume the chroma tokens for non-text fills or for solid `bg-destructive` are unaffected тАФ only Badge and Button were rewired to the AA tokens.

    Known gaps left intentionally for now: (1) Card boundary uses `border-border/70` (light, ~1.18:1) / `dark:border-border` (dark, ~1.47:1) тАФ under SC 1.4.11's 3:1, but the boundary is decorative and the current visual treatment was preferred over the heavier stone-400-grade border that strict compliance would require. (2) `text-destructive` used as text in `Alert` destructive, `DropdownMenu` destructive items, `Form` error messages, and `Connection-banner` still pulls the chroma `--destructive` directly. A follow-up could add a `--destructive-text` token (theme-specific) or migrate these to `text-destructive-aa`.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui`
  - No code changes required for consumers тАФ the variant names and APIs are the same. If you previously hand-replicated Badge's `bg-success/10 text-success` pattern in your own code, switch your local pattern to `text-success-soft-fg` (or define an equivalent token) so it clears AA.
  - Re-screenshot any visual snapshots/regressions covering Badge `destructive` / `success` / `warning` / `info` / `muted (dark)` and Button `destructive` тАФ colors shifted intentionally.

#### meter-row тАФ `label` is now optional; bare-bar mode skips the label row

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `MeterRow.label` is widened from required `ReactNode` to optional `ReactNode | undefined`. The component now renders the label-row container (`flex items-center justify-between gap-2 text-sm` + the outer `space-y-1` gap) only when `label != null || valueLabel !== undefined`. Previously the label row was unconditional тАФ even `label={null}` left ~24px of phantom blank space above the bar because the empty `<span>` still picked up `text-sm` line-height inside the flex container. The new behavior: pass a `label` (and optionally `valueLabel`) to keep the existing labeled layout, or omit both to render a bare progress bar that sits flush against its parent. Useful for compact list-row indicators where the count is shown separately above, KPI metric tiles where the label lives in the surrounding wrapper, and any case where the consumer renders its own label/value pair around the bar. Existing callers that pass a `label` are unchanged. Closes the gap that forced consumers to keep hand-rolled `<div className="h-X bg-muted"><div className="h-full bg-{tone}" style={{ width }} /></div>` patterns whenever they wanted a tone-aware progress bar without `MeterRow`'s built-in label slot.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui`
  - Replace remaining hand-rolled tone-aware progress bars (`<div bg-muted><div bg-success/info/warning style={{ width }}>`) with `<MeterRow value={pct} tone="success" size="sm" />` (omit `label` for bare bars). Pick `size` to match the original height: `sm`=h-1, `default`=h-1.5, `lg`=h-2.

#### package тАФ npm package renamed to `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui`

- change: renamed
- breaking: yes
- details: The npm package was renamed from `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui` so the package name matches the `lucid-ui` repo it ships from. The previous `@pandaworks-sw/ui` package on GitHub Packages stops receiving updates at v0.1.4; v0.2.0 is the first release under the new name. No source code, exports, public API, or styling tokens change тАФ only the package name and subpath import paths.
- action:
  - In `package.json`, replace `"@pandaworks-sw/ui": "<old>"` with `"@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui": "^0.2.0"`.
  - Rewrite every import: `from '@pandaworks-sw/ui'` тЖТ `from '@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui'`. Stylesheet too: `@import "@pandaworks-sw/ui/styles.css"` тЖТ `@import "@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui/styles.css"`.
  - Update Tailwind v4 source line: `@source "../node_modules/@pandaworks-sw/ui"` тЖТ `@source "../node_modules/@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui"`.
  - `.npmrc` is unchanged тАФ the scope `@pandaworks-sw` is the same; only the package within the scope changed.
  - Run `pnpm install` to refresh the lockfile.

#### multi-stat-card тАФ new component

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: New `MultiStatCard` component renders 2+ related stat metrics inside a single card with dividers between cells. Each cell mirrors `StatCard` (icon + label, animated headline value, optional hint and delta chip) and reuses the same design tokens, headline auto-shrink heuristic, and delta-chip rendering. Per-cell shape is `MultiStatCardItem` (subset of `StatCardProps` minus `className`): `icon`, `label`, `value`, `prefix`, `suffix`, `decimals`, `formatter`, `hint`, `delta`, `deltaTone`. `orientation` (default `"horizontal"`) controls divider direction (`divide-x` vs `divide-y`); cells use `flex-1 min-w-0` so widths split evenly and long values still shrink. Use it for funnel metrics (Total / In Progress / Completed), date pairs (Form Start / Submission Due), or any "metrics that belong together" cluster you don't want broken across separate tiles. Internally, the shared helpers (`DELTA_VARIANT`, `DELTA_ICON`, `getHeadlineSizeClass`, `getRenderedLength`, `DeltaTone`) were extracted from `stat-card.tsx` into a new sibling `stat-card/stat-card-shared.ts` so both components stay in sync тАФ no behavior change for existing `StatCard` usage.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - Replace ad-hoc multi-cell `Card`s (e.g. a `Card` containing a 3-column grid of label/number divs) with `<MultiStatCard items={[...]} />`
  - When mixing into a grid of single `StatCard` tiles, give the `MultiStatCard` a `col-span` equal to its cell count so the row stays balanced (e.g. `className="sm:col-span-2 xl:col-span-3"` for a 3-cell card inside an `xl:grid-cols-6` grid)

#### stat-card тАФ `value` prop now accepts strings

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `StatCard`'s `value` prop is widened from `number` to `number | string`. Numeric values keep the existing `AnimatedNumber` path (with `prefix` / `suffix` / `decimals` / `formatter` adornments). String values render as-is тАФ useful for already-formatted or non-numeric tiles such as `"N/A"`, `"тАФ"`, `"Active"`, `"Enterprise"`, `"8 / 10"`, or pre-formatted currency. `prefix`, `suffix`, `decimals`, and `formatter` are ignored when `value` is a string (strings are assumed already-formatted). The `delta` / `deltaTone` chip and `hint` continue to work for both forms.
- action:
  - No migration needed for existing numeric usage.
  - When showing a non-animated label (status, tier, ratio, "N/A"), pass the string directly instead of building a custom `Card` wrapper.

#### stat-card тАФ headline auto-shrinks for long values

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: The headline class steps down based on the rendered value's length so long values still fit inside the tile without overflowing or wrapping awkwardly: `тЙд10 chars тЖТ text-xl`, `тЙд16 тЖТ text-lg`, `тЙд24 тЖТ text-base`, `>24 тЖТ text-sm`. Length is computed from the string for string values, from `formatter(value)` when a formatter is provided, and from `prefix + integer digits + decimals + suffix` for plain numbers. Pure CSS тАФ no `ResizeObserver`, no measurement, no extra render тАФ so the cost is zero. The headline wrapper also gets `min-w-0` so it can shrink inside narrower grid columns. Short numeric values (the common case, e.g. `value={42}` or `value={73} suffix="%"`) keep `text-xl` and look identical to before.
- action:
  - No migration needed.
  - If a value is now appearing smaller than expected, count the rendered length (string, or `prefix + digits + decimals + suffix`) and compare against the thresholds. If the value is supposed to be a hero number, the tile is the wrong primitive тАФ use a plain `Card` with custom typography.

### 2026-05-01

#### avatar тАФ `getInitialName` utility + `AvatarFallback` auto-converts full names

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: New helper `getInitialName(name: string): string` exported from `@pandaworks-sw/ui` (and from the registry's internal `@/lib` alias тАФ see `packages/registry/src/lib/get-initial-name.ts`). Pass any string тАФ including a full multi-word name тАФ to `<AvatarFallback>` and it now renders a 2-character monogram automatically: `"John Doe" тЖТ "JD"`, `"Devi Marasinghe" тЖТ "DM"` (first letter of first + last word), `"Eli" тЖТ "EL"` (first 2 chars of single-word names), `""` тЖТ empty. Already-short strings such as `"JD"` or `"+3"` pass through unchanged so existing call sites that pre-compute initials keep working. Callers that previously hand-wrote a `getInitials` helper (e.g. `app-shell.tsx`) can drop it and pass `user.name` directly.
- action:
  - Optional cleanup: replace ad-hoc `getInitials(user.name)` helpers with `<AvatarFallback>{user.name}</AvatarFallback>` or `import { getInitialName } from '@pandaworks-sw/ui'` for non-Avatar contexts (aria-labels, sorting).

#### avatar тАФ `AvatarFallback` colorize default flipped to `true`

- change: modified
- breaking: yes
- details: The `colorize` prop on `AvatarFallback` now defaults to `true`. `<AvatarFallback>John Doe</AvatarFallback>` renders a deterministically colored monogram out of the box (AтАУZ map to 26 evenly spaced OKLCH hues; digits and other characters fall back to a deterministic hash; foreground is white in both light and dark mode). Pass `colorize={false}` to opt back into the muted (`bg-muted`) look тАФ useful for `+N` overflow tiles, system placeholders, and other non-name fallbacks. The `+N` overflow tile inside `AvatarGroup` (`packages/registry/registry/default/avatar-group/avatar-group.tsx`) was updated to set `colorize={false}` so it keeps its neutral background.
- action:
  - If a project relied on the previous gray default for arbitrary, non-name text content (e.g. `"+N"` chips, system tiles, single-character placeholders), add `colorize={false}` to those `AvatarFallback` instances.
  - Name-based avatars need no change тАФ they get the colorized look automatically.
  - The previous opt-in form `<AvatarFallback colorize>JD</AvatarFallback>` still works (just redundant now).
